title: How Your Retina Detects Light
script_summary: Exploring how photoreceptors in the retina convert light into vision.

segments.voiceover: Photoreceptors convert photons into neural signals your brain turns into vision.
But how does this incredible transformation happen?
Inside your retina, two main types of photoreceptors, rods and cones, play key roles.
Rods help us see in low light, while cones allow us to perceive color.
This delicate process sends signals to your brain, creating the vivid world you experience.
Curious about more secrets of your body? Follow for more fascinating insights!
